transformation_type: directed ortho lithiation/metallation of arenes and heteroarenes
mechanism_type: complex-induced proximity effect guided deprotonation by alkyllithiums
reactant_pattern_text: aromatic substrate bearing directing metalation group + organolithium
common_conditions: typically -78 °C to room temperature followed by electrophile quench
description_short: Directed ortho lithiation method using a coordinating substituent to guide metalation to the ortho position.

mechanism_type: base-promoted rearrangement of quaternary ammonium salts via nitrogen ylide
common_conditions: strong base or ylide-generation conditions; competition with Stevens rearrangement
description_short: Rearrangement of benzylic ammonium ylides to ortho-substituted benzylamines under strongly basic conditions.

transformation_type: Pd/Cu-catalyzed coupling of terminal alkynes with aryl or vinyl halides
mechanism_type: oxidative addition-transmetalation-reductive elimination involving copper acetylides
common_conditions: room temperature to reflux under Pd/Cu catalysis with amine base
description_short: Cross-coupling reaction joining terminal alkynes with aryl or vinyl electrophiles under palladium and copper catalysis.

transformation_type: [2+2] cycloaddition of ketenes with alkenes, imines, or carbonyls
mechanism_type: concerted or Lewis acid mediated ketene cycloaddition
common_conditions: thermal or Lewis acid promoted ketene generation and capture
description_short: Thermal or Lewis acid assisted [2+2] cycloaddition chemistry of ketenes that furnishes four-membered rings.

transformation_type: reduction or aza-Wittig functionalization of organic azides via phosphines
mechanism_type: phosphine attack on azide to phosphazide and iminophosphorane intermediates
product_pattern_text: amine or aza-Wittig derived imine/amide product
common_conditions: usually mild phosphine-mediated azide reduction or trapping of iminophosphorane
description_short: Reaction of azides with phosphines to form iminophosphoranes that can be hydrolyzed to amines or intercepted in aza-Wittig/ligation chemistry.
